title: nigella degenii subsp. minor
intro: Nigella degenii subsp. minor, also known as the lesser Balkan nigella or lesser fiddleneck, is a plant species in the borage family (Boraginaceae) that is native to the Balkan Peninsula. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it effective in treating wounds and skin conditions such as eczema and acne. The health benefits of Nigella degenii subsp. minor include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and boost the immune system, making it a valuable herbal remedy for various health issues. The plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be consumed to reap its medicinal and health benefits.
